justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Authentication per consumer type correctly designed — JWT for mobile, API key for partners with distinct rate limit tiers. (2) Internal traffic separation fully addressed via network-level isolation with private listener and mTLS. (3) Business logic explicitly excluded from gateway in multiple places ('Does NOT contain business logic', 'Do NOT put business logic in the gateway'). (4) SPOF risk identified as critical with horizontal scaling mitigation. (5) Webhook delivery for partners addressed with dedicated Webhook Dispatcher component including retry, HMAC signing, and dead letter queue. (6) flexibility_points (4) and rigidity_points (3) present and well-reasoned. (7) Technology recommendation covers Kong, Envoy, AWS API Gateway, Traefik with explicit build-vs-buy guidance favoring existing solutions.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough three-listener topology cleanly separating mobile, partner, and internal traffic — goes beyond minimum requirements
Webhook Dispatcher is properly decoupled from the gateway via event bus with at-least-once delivery guarantees, HMAC signing, and exponential backoff retry
Explicit and repeated exclusion of business logic from gateway, including pitfalls section warning against it
Phased migration strategy (passthrough → enforce per-service → remove legacy) demonstrates practical implementation awareness
Trade-offs section is exceptionally well-structured with alternatives_considered for each decision
Build vs buy clearly addressed — recommends proven gateway products and explicitly warns against building custom
Rigidity points are honest and include intentional rigidity (identity header contract) which shows architectural maturity
Fail-open vs fail-closed trade-off for rate limiting is a nuanced decision that most outputs miss
